categories.name: Meal Services

questions.text: Does staff distribute and serve food under sanitary conditions?

observations: Plates’ eating surfaces do not contact staff clothing.
Cups/glasses handled only on the outside.
Utensils handled by their handles only.
Hands kept away from hair and face when handling food.
Hair restraints (hairnet/hat/beard cover) worn per Food Code.

categories.name: Infection Control

questions.text: Did the facility provide a safe, sanitary, and comfortable environment and help prevent the development and transmission of communicable diseases and infections?

observations: Staff free of open skin areas/signs of infection/illness.
Hand hygiene performed between residents after skin or secretion contact.

categories.name: Dignity

questions.text: Does the facility promote care for residents in a manner and in an environment that maintains or enhances each resident’s dignity and respect in full recognition of his or her individuality?

observations: Residents seated at same time receive meals together (or served upon arrival for open seating).
Napkins and nondisposable dishware/cutlery provided.
Clothing protectors used honoring resident wishes.
Scraping plates occurs after all residents at a table finish.
Assistive stance is seated next to resident, not standing over.
Staff converse with residents they assist, not with other staff.
Residents given adequate unrushed time to eat.
Communication is polite, respectful, and preserves confidentiality.
Resident requests addressed timely.

categories.name: Homelike Environment

questions.text: Did the facility provide a homelike dining environment?

observations: Avoidance of institutional practices (e.g., trays in dining room).
Medication timing/practices do not disrupt dining.
Pain medications given prior to meals when indicated.
Food is not routinely used as a vehicle for meds unless appropriate.

categories.name: Resident Self-Determination / Preferences

questions.text: Does the facility honor the resident’s right to make choices about aspects of his/her life in the facility that are significant to the resident?

observations: If resident refuses dining room/meal/items, staff assess reasons and respond.
Resident choice of when and where to eat is honored.
Food offered reflects individual preferences.
Staff can describe protocols for where/when residents eat and how preferences reach dietary.

categories.name: Dining Assistance

questions.text: Does the facility provide assistance with meals, assisting with hydration, and nutritional provisions throughout the day?

observations: Cueing/prompting/assistance provided to maintain or improve eating ability.
Process for in-room meals and assistance is effective.
Adequate staff presence during dining.
Dependent residents receive timely assistance; if not, assess sufficiency of nursing staff.

categories.name: Assistive Devices

questions.text: Does the facility provide resident with assistive devices if needed?

observations: Adaptive utensils/dishware are provided and in use for residents who require them.

categories.name: Positioning

questions.text: Is the resident positioned correctly to provide care and services that promote the highest practical well-being?

observations: Wheelchairs fit under tables; residents can access food without difficulty.
Residents seated in proper alignment for safe eating.

categories.name: Dietary Needs (Allergies, Intolerances, Preferences)

questions.text: Are residents receiving food that accommodates resident allergies, intolerances, and preferences?

observations: Staff can explain how special diets/allergies/preferences are identified and implemented.

categories.name: Paid Feeding Assistants — Eligibility & Supervision

questions.text: Are residents selected based on an IDT assessment? Are paid feeding assistants supervised or used in accordance to State law?

observations: Only residents without complicated feeding problems are assigned to paid feeding assistants.
Paid feeding assistants are adequately supervised and match resident needs.
If non-PFA staff show technique issues, consider CNA proficiency review (F726).

categories.name: Paid Feeding Assistants — Training

questions.text: Have the paid feeding assistants completed a State-approved training program prior to working in the facility?

observations: Training completion documented before independent work.

categories.name: Food and Drink Quality — Palatability, Temperature, Appearance

questions.text: Does the facility serve meals that conserve nutritive value, flavor, and appearance, and are palatable, attractive, and a safe and appetizing temperature?

observations: Pureed/mechanically altered diets plated as separate entrée items when appropriate.
Food placement, colors, textures match resident needs (e.g., vision/swallowing deficits).
Residents interviewed about palatability and temperature.
If concerns, test tray used near time last resident begins eating on the unit farthest from kitchen or affected unit.

categories.name: Nutritional Status

questions.text: Do the residents maintain acceptable parameters of nutritional status unless the resident’s clinical condition demonstrates otherwise or resident preferences indicate otherwise?

observations: Meal intake patterns and interventions align with maintaining nutritional status.

categories.name: Drinks and Other Liquids

questions.text: Does the facility provide drinks including water and other liquids consistent with residents’ needs and preferences?

observations: Fluids available when requested and per care plan.
Preferences for beverages honored.

categories.name: Food Substitutes

questions.text: Does the facility offer an appealing option of similar nutritive value to residents who refuse food being served?

observations: When a meal/item is refused, staff determine reason and offer substitute of equal nutritive value or preferred alternative.
Staff can describe available substitutes for the observed meal.

categories.name: Therapeutic Diets

questions.text: Are residents receiving therapeutic diets as prescribed?

observations: Observed trays match prescribed therapeutic diets.
Tray cards/dietary identifiers align with orders; records confirm prescriptions.

categories.name: Lighting

questions.text: Does the facility provide one or more rooms designated for dining that are well lighted?
Does the facility provide adequate and comfortable lighting levels in the dining areas?

observations: Illumination is task-appropriate with minimal glare.
Lighting supports independent function and eating tasks.
Residents report lighting is comfortable and adequate.

categories.name: Ventilation

questions.text: Does the facility provide one or more rooms designated for dining that is well ventilated?

observations: Efficient ventilation and good air circulation.
Acceptable temperature and humidity.
No floor-level drafts; smoke/odors adequately removed.

categories.name: Sound Levels

questions.text: Does the facility provide comfortable sound levels in the dining areas?

observations: Conversation possible without raised voices.
Background noise does not prevent residents from being heard or concentrating.
Residents can control or avoid unwanted noise.

categories.name: Comfortable and Safe Temperatures

questions.text: Does the facility maintain comfortable and safe temperature levels in the dining areas?

observations: Ambient temperature maintained in a narrow comfort range (reference 71–81°F).
Staff actions observed when residents report hot/cold.
Staff can explain how temperatures are set/maintained and measures when out of range.

categories.name: Furnishings

questions.text: Are the dining areas adequately furnished to meet residents’ physical and social needs?

observations: Table heights allow easy visibility and access to food.
Chairs of varying sizes; furnishings structurally sound.
Wheelchairs can fit under tables.

categories.name: Space

questions.text: Do the dining areas have sufficient space to accommodate all dining activities?

observations: Residents can enter/exit independently without moving others.
Rapid egress possible in emergencies.
Staff can reach any resident quickly (e.g., choking).
No crowding observed.

categories.name: Frequency and Timing of Meals

questions.text: Does the facility provide at least three meals daily at regular times comparable to mealtimes in the community or in accordance with residents’ needs?

Does the facility provide meals with no greater than a 14-hour lapse between the evening meal and breakfast, or 16 hours with approval of a resident group and provision of a substantial evening snack?

observations: Meal availability beyond posted times when needed.
Process if a resident misses service period.
Snacks available (types/timing) and alternative meals offered at non-traditional times consistent with care plans.
Evening-to-breakfast lapse ≤14 hours (or ≤16 hours with resident group approval and substantial evening snack).

categories.name: Food and Nutrition Services Staffing

questions.text: Does the facility provide sufficient staff to safely and effectively carry out the functions of the food and nutrition services, including preparing and serving meals, in the scheduled time frames?

observations: Staffing levels support timely preparation and service for the observed meal.
Delays or missed assistance are not attributable to understaffing.
